_note: Fixture for eval 3 — Feature with no Proposed Tasks checklist where one AC (chunked ZIP export for large datasets) is significantly more complex than the others and must become its own separate Task.

feature.title: Feature: Settlement CSV export
feature.body:
## Goal
Allow merchants to download their settlement data as a CSV file so they can perform offline analysis and import into accounting tools.

## Bounded Context
Settlement

## User Stories
- As a Merchant, I want to download my settlements as a CSV file so that I can analyse them in a spreadsheet.
- As a Merchant, I want any active filters to be applied to my CSV export so that I only export the data I can see on screen.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: A merchant can trigger a CSV download from the settlement list UI; the file contains all columns visible in the list.
- AC2: When filters (e.g. date range, status) are active, the exported CSV reflects those filters — only matching settlements are included.
- AC3: Exports containing more than 10,000 rows are chunked into multiple CSV files and delivered to the merchant as a single ZIP archive; a progress indicator is shown while the archive is being prepared.

## Edge Cases
- Exporting zero results produces an empty CSV with the header row only.
- Chunking boundary falls exactly on 10,000 rows: the archive contains two files, each up to 10,000 rows.
- If the ZIP preparation exceeds 60 seconds, the merchant receives an email link to download the archive instead.

## Domain Events
- `SettlementExportRequested` — emitted when a merchant initiates an export.
- `SettlementExportReady` — emitted when the export file or archive is ready for download.
